expected_structure_scholarly.khilas: supplemental (Vālakhilya + others), not in Śākala padapāṭha count

census_status: PARTIAL — local witnesses characterized; missing-witness universe documented but NOT exhaustively searched this session
method_note: Grounded in files actually present under knowledge/downloads plus established Rigveda textual scholarship. No remote searches performed this turn; missing-witness entries are documented from scholarship with discovery leads, NOT verified as acquired.

witnesses.name: Aufrecht edition (GRETIL TEI)
witnesses.stemma_role: primary editorial base; all modern digital RV derive from this printed edition
witnesses.notes: 21,035 <l> (pada-level) elements = 10,552 verses. THE reference digital text.

witnesses.name: VedaWeb TEI (Lubotsky + VNH)
witnesses.critical: Independently transcribed diplomatic text (Lubotsky) + Aufrecht-based layer (VNH)
witnesses.stemma_role: Lubotsky = genuinely independent scholarly transcription. VNH layer = DERIVATIVE of Aufrecht (RV-W01).
witnesses.notes: Books 1-10, 10,552 stanzas. Contains BOTH an independent reading (Lubotsky) and a re-encoding of Aufrecht (VNH). Prior '3-witness' comparison counted VNH separately, inflating independence.

witnesses.name: Padapāṭha (GRETIL TEI)
witnesses.critical: Word-divided śikṣā tradition (separate textual witness, not a commentary)
witnesses.stemma_role: independent textual tradition (pada vs saṃhitā), used for sandhi/phonology validation
witnesses.notes: Word-division tradition; validates pada boundaries and sandhi.

witnesses.name: Khilāni / Khilas (GRETIL TEI, Tokunaga)
witnesses.stemma_role: supplement; not part of the 1028-sūkta Śākala core
witnesses.notes: 556 verses on disk (prior summary over-counted as 1920; actual <l>=1314 lines).

witnesses.name: Max Müller, 'Rig-Veda Sanhita' (Archive.org OCR)
witnesses.stemma_role: INDEPENDENT editorial base vs Aufrecht — would add real diversity IF readable
witnesses.notes: OCR is destructively garbled (encoding damage). Cannot currently be collated. Re-acquisition of a clean scan or native text needed.

witnesses.name: Chowkhamba Sanskrit Series Office, 1973 (Uma Shankar Sharma)
witnesses.stemma_role: publisher reprint — likely derivative of Aufrecht; low independence
witnesses.license: In copyright (post-1957 Indian edition) — verify before redistribution
witnesses.notes: OCR quality poor; primarily useful as a Devanagari sanity check, not as an independent critical witness.

witnesses.name: Sontakke / Vaidika Saṃśodhana Maṇḍala edition (Part 4, Mandalas 6-8)
editors: N. S. Sontakke et al. (Vaidika Saṃśodhana Maṇḍala, Poona)
witnesses.critical: Critical edition WITH Sāyaṇa bhāṣya; important independent editorial tradition
witnesses.stemma_role: scholarly critical edition lineage — higher authority than Chowkhamba
witnesses.notes: Only Part 4 on disk (Mandas 6-8). Rest of the 5-part set (Mandas 1-5, 9-10) NOT acquired.

witnesses.name: Sāyaṇa Bhāṣya (multiple OCR scans)
witnesses.critical: Commentary (not a saṃhitā witness, but authoritative interpretive tradition)
witnesses.license: Public domain (commentary text); print eds may differ
witnesses.notes: Three overlapping OCR sets of Sāyaṇa. None clean. No complete native-Unicode Sāyaṇa yet.

witnesses.name: Ramtek / KKSU Śrī Raghuveda collection (OCR)
witnesses.notes: Devanagari; useful for script-fidelity cross-check only.

witnesses.name: Translations (Wilson English, Hindi, Gujarati)
witnesses.stemma_role: secondary; useful for semantic validation only
witnesses.notes: Wilson 1850s English; Hindi/Gujarati partial. Not collation-grade for the Sanskrit text.

witnesses.name: Rigveda_api.json (V33nom) sample
witnesses.stemma_role: NOT a complete witness; subset only
witnesses.notes: Only 179 sample verses. Cannot serve as a collation base.

witness_universe_missing.name: Aufrecht 1st ed. (1861, Bonn)
witness_universe_missing.why: earlier editorial state; predates 1863 metrical restoration

witness_universe_missing.name: Oldenberg & Schmidt, 'Rig-Veda: Textkritische und exegetische Noten' (1909-13) + Sāyaṇa
witness_universe_missing.why: major critical apparatus with Sāyaṇa; independent editorial judgment

witness_universe_missing.name: Sāyaṇa Bhāṣya — complete native Unicode (e.g., Muktabodha / SARIT TEI)
witness_universe_missing.why: authoritative commentary, no clean digital text on disk

witness_universe_missing.name: Bāṣkala-padapāṭha (extant portion of Bāṣkala recension)
witness_universe_missing.why: only surviving testimony of a second RV recension

witness_universe_missing.name: Native Unicode Devanagari saṃhitā (e.g., Gita Press / Arya Sahitya Mandir / IIT/KSU)
Complete Sontakke VSM edition (Parts 1-5)
witness_universe_missing.why: Part 4 only on disk; rest missing

witness_universe_missing.name: BORI / MNL (Mumbai) critical-apparatus holdings
witness_universe_missing.why: institutional critical materials not yet enumerated

derivative_of_aufrecht: most publisher reprints (Chowkhamba W06, Ramtek W09, Gita Press)
stemma.key_finding: Only TWO genuinely independent editorial bases are currently collatable from disk: Aufrecht (1863) and Lubotsky. VNH and all publisher reprints descend from Aufrecht. Max Müller (independent 3rd base) is on disk but OCR-destroyed.

falsification_of_prior_claim.prior_claim: Rigveda text is remarkably stable across witnesses; differences are editorial (accents, boundaries, diacritics), not genuine variants.
falsification_of_prior_claim.reason: The '3-witness' comparison conflated VNH (Aufrecht derivative) with an independent witness. True independent bases compared = Aufrecht vs Lubotsky only (2). Max Müller (the classic 3rd independent base) was OCR-unusable and excluded.
required_before_certification: Recover a clean Max Müller text (or other independent 19th-c. edition) and collate it
Acquire & parse a native-Unicode Devanagari saṃhitā

acquisition_queue.target: Max Müller clean text (re-OCR or native)

queries: Archive.org advancedsearch by editor (Max Muller, Oldenberg)

findings.result: Downloaded _djvu.txt. CONFIRMED it is Oldenberg's German text-critical PROLEGOMENA + notes, NOT the edited samhita text. Sanskrit appears only as quoted examples. Value: critical-apparatus citations, not collatable full text.
PDF-only (302 MB). Critical notes for books 1-6. Valuable apparatus but no machine-readable samhita text from metadata endpoint.
Downloaded _djvu.txt (3MB). DEVANAGARI OCR IS GARBLED (916k Devanagari chars, 0 recoverable IAST). NOT collatable. Confirmed unusable 2nd time.
Sacred Books of the East translation vol; _djvu.txt is English translation, no IAST samhita text lines.

discovery_sessions.conclusion: No collatable 3rd independent samhita base obtained this session. Existing collatable independent bases remain F-AUFRECHT + F-LUBOTSKY. Gap is EVIDENCED, not assumed.
next_attempts: SARIT/Muktabodha native-Unicode Sayana (needs different access path)
Clean Max Muller via HathiTrust full text if accessible
GRETIL alternate mirror for sayana unicode
